display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
loopSettings
deviceToken, dosingStrategy
override presets, guards

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
TherapySettings
dia, carbsHr, delay, GI variants
timezone, units, perGIValues
loopSettings, enteredBy
isDefault, startDate

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
TargetRangeSchedule
merged low + high into
time-of-day target ranges

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Pharmacokinetics vs preferences
DIA and carb absorption are drug
properties; timezone and units are
user display preferences. Bundling
them forces a full profile push
when only one concern changes.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Independent schedule lifecycles
Basal rates change when a pump is
adjusted. Carb ratios change when
diet shifts. ISF changes with
activity patterns. Coupling them
means every tweak overwrites the
entire profile.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Target ranges are clinical policy
Target ranges are set by
endocrinologists and change on
clinic visits. They should not
be bundled with self-adjusted
basal rates or carb ratios.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Audit granularity
With a monolith, 'profile updated'
tells you nothing. Decomposed
records show exactly which
parameter changed, when, and by
which device or app.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Query efficiency
A connector fetching basal rates
for pump sync should not load
carb ratios, sensitivity factors,
and Loop device tokens.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Loop settings are device config
Device tokens, dosing strategies,
and override presets are APS
integration concerns, not therapy
parameters. Mixing them means
a Loop settings sync can
overwrite clinical therapy data.
